title: Support choice in-field differentiation
summary: Make support choice felt during outings rather than mostly visible on map/station chrome.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: At least three support choices create distinct in-field reading or feedback on existing routes with no inventory expansion.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Normalize support-selection persistence and station echo so support state remains small, deterministic, and save-safe.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_350_target: Keep lane 1 systems-small: add one shared station-label seam for outing support, expose that exact label in field-station debug state, and refresh stale support-row runtime smoke expectations for the current guided world-map focus rule.

baseline.failing: `npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "route marker|outing support|locked route-marker|route board to tundra|coastal scrub and can hand"` currently fails five stale initial map-focus assertions because the newer guided focus rule opens on the active outing target instead of the old current-location fallback.

main_acceptance: the visible routes-page support row and debug field-station state use one shared station-label mapping for `HAND LENS`, `NOTE TABS`, `PLACE TAB`, and `ROUTE MARKER`
focused coverage proves locked `route-marker` saves echo `hand-lens` / `HAND LENS`
focused coverage proves route-marker activation echoes `route-marker` / `ROUTE MARKER` and still points the world map at the active outing target
the stale runtime-smoke initial map-focus expectations are updated to the current guided active-target behavior
no save schema, support order, notice copy, route-marker behavior, hand-lens retargeting, note-tabs/place-tab behavior, station layout, new UI, geometry, route definitions, or science copy changes

main_result.summary: Added a shared station-label helper for outing support, reused it in the visible routes-page row, exposed `fieldStation.selectedOutingSupportLabel` through debug state, and refreshed stale support-row runtime-smoke map-focus expectations for the guided active-target rule.
verified: `npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "route marker|outing support|locked route-marker|route board to tundra|coastal scrub and can hand"`
non_goals_preserved: no save schema or support order change
no route-marker behavior, hand-lens retargeting, note-tabs/place-tab behavior, station layout, route definition, geometry, new UI, or science-copy change

critic_result.summary: Confirmed the shared station-label seam is pure, visible route-page and debug labels share the same mapping, locked support saves cannot show stale route-marker labels, route-marker activation still points to the active outing target, and the guided active-target map-focus expectation refresh did not change support behavior.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Clarify support labels and microcopy so kids understand lens/tabs/place/marker differences in five words or fewer per cue.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Add small visual affordances for support-relevant clue reading only where an existing route lacks a physical cue.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Implement support-specific in-field consequences across route families using existing Route v2 seams, not a new loadout model.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_353_target: Reuse the existing in-field NOTEBOOK J chip as a tiny support-specific cue seam for note-tabs and place-tab while preserving hand-lens inspect retargeting as the only support that changes nearest inspectable selection.
current_gap: Hand lens already changes in-field clue targeting and can mark the chip as support-biased.
Note-tabs and place-tab are currently strongest on the station TODAY wrap, so they are less felt during the outing itself.
Route-marker should remain map/travel-facing for this pass.
recommended_behavior: keep hand-lens behavior unchanged: notebook-fit retargeting, active-clue preference, and LENS CLUE inspect-bubble copy remain hand-lens-only
for note-tabs on an active Route v2 outing, make the existing in-field chip progress/notebook-facing using active request progress state and the existing support-biased variant
for place-tab on an active Route v2 outing, make the existing in-field chip place-question-facing with compact chip copy and the existing support-biased variant
keep route-marker map/travel-facing and prove it does not gain inspect retargeting or notebook-chip behavior accidentally
main_acceptance: at least three support choices feel distinct in-field or during route travel without a new UI surface: hand-lens, note-tabs, and place-tab
non-hand-lens supports do not change nearest inspectable selection, inspect-bubble resource notes, route progress, or filed-note behavior
support-specific chip behavior is covered by focused field-request-controller tests and at most one representative runtime-smoke update if debug-state coverage is needed
no station layout, support order, save schema, route definitions, route filing behavior, world geometry, authored science/copy, nursery behavior, new HUD, inventory, or loadout model changes
verification: focused `runtime-smoke` slice only if `render_game_to_text()` chip state is changed beyond controller-level unit coverage

main_result.summary: Reused the existing in-field `NOTEBOOK J` chip as the support-specific Route v2 cue seam: hand lens keeps inspect retargeting and `LENS CLUE`, note-tabs now shows active route progress, place-tab shows compact `Place Question` chip copy, and route-marker keeps default map/travel-facing behavior.
guardrails_preserved: no save schema, support order, station layout, route definitions, route filing behavior, world geometry, authored science/copy, nursery behavior, new HUD, inventory, or loadout model changes
non-hand-lens supports do not change nearest inspectable selection or inspect-bubble notebook-fit behavior
route-marker does not gain hand-lens-style inspect retargeting or support-biased notebook-chip behavior

critic_result.summary: Confirmed the lane-4 implementation stays in the existing Route v2 hint/targeting seam: hand lens remains the only inspect-retargeting support, note-tabs shows route progress, place-tab shows compact place-question chip copy, and route-marker remains map/travel-facing with default chip behavior.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.

lane_2_scout_refinement.focus: Shorten support-toggle notice copy so each cue is five words or fewer, without changing support behavior.

main_351_scope.required_change: Update only `getOutingSupportNoticeText(...)` and its focused test expectations.
main_351_scope.shared_file_reason: The pure support notice-copy helper currently lives in lane-4-owned src/engine/field-request-controller.ts; lane 2 may touch only that copy helper and no route/support behavior.

non_goals: No support selection order, save normalization, route-marker behavior, hand-lens retargeting, note-tabs/place-tab route behavior, inspect-bubble label, or notebook chip changes.
No station layout, route-board layout, world-map/corridor behavior, save schema, renderer geometry, route definitions, or new support UI.
No new loadout model, inventory row, or tutorial panel.

lane_2_main_result.guardrail_result: Only the support notice-copy helper and focused test changed; support behavior, save behavior, station layout, route behavior, renderer geometry, and new UI surfaces stayed untouched.

checks: The implementation changed only the support notice-copy helper and focused test.
All four notices are five words or fewer and exact-copy tested.
Support selection, route targeting, save behavior, station layout, world-map behavior, renderer geometry, route definitions, and new UI surfaces stayed untouched.
